key_insight: A hierarchical NMPC scheme with neural thermal models can jointly optimize BEV cabin comfort and battery safety while reducing HVAC/BTM energy use.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical control architecture for extending electric vehicle range without sacrificing passenger comfort or thermal constraints.
method: Supervisory NMPC optimizes long-horizon trade-offs between energy consumption and cabin temperature tracking.
Lower-layer NMPC applies local corrections to handle disturbances and model uncertainty.
Data-driven neural network models predict the nonlinear coupled thermal dynamics for both control layers.
evidence_signal: The approach was validated in a high-fidelity BEV thermal simulator and benchmarked against an industrial rule-based controller over the WLTC driving cycle, showing lower energy use while meeting comfort and battery limits.
watch_item: Performance depends on the fidelity of the learned thermal models and simulator-to-real-world transfer under varying weather, traffic, and aging conditions.
paper_title: Thermal management optimization of Battery Electric Vehicles via hierarchical NMPC with CMO-trained Neural Models
